body_txt: So, you're watching this show where someone appears to be The Cast Show Off , then you notice that their hands aren't matching the notes at all. Sometimes, to the point where they didn't even try. Or, perhaps someone is talking about music and it turns out it's just musical Techno Babble . When an author/artist/filmmaker/game designer/etc. tries to create a work featuring musical instruments or music as a plot point and they are not an experienced musician themselves, they will usually lack the knowledge necessary to correctly depict that instrument or how that instrument functions. To people not familiar with music, it doesn't bother them, likely because it's not relevant to the plot, but to musicians it's obvious. It also mostly applies to instrumental music, because not everyone knows the technical skills and what it looks like to play an instrument, and instruments can be easily substituted in on the soundtrack because of the uniformity of sound. Guitars sound much more like each other than voices do. This can manifest in a work in three ways:  Note: Lip-synching does not apply here, because most people know how to lip-synch, and music videos are ( almost ) always the voices of the artist, with or without pitch correction or cobbling multiple takes. Also, some Dunning Kruger afflicted beginner musicians may not be aware of things like nonstandard tunings on guitars, transpose buttons on keyboards, or alternative key mappings. See also Music Genre Dissonance . Impractical Musical Instrument Skills can overlap when it involves playing techniques that are impossible in real life.
